name: problem-body
text:
- Interactive systems do not model physical movement reliably across heterogeneous sensors.
- Vision-only and isolated wearable approaches lack synchronized, standardized signals.
- This limits analysis of posture, load, symmetry and exertion.
textPreview: - Interactive systems do not model physical movement reliably across heterogeneous sensors. | - Vision-only and isolated wearable approac...

name: method-body
text:
- Camera + YOLO: body-pose estimation and dumbbell detection.
- ESP32 + IMU: motion, orientation and stability telemetry.
- Garmin Venu 3: heart-rate and physiological context.
- Fusion pipeline synchronizes, calibrates and exports normalized JSON/JSONL features.
textPreview: - Camera + YOLO: body-pose estimation and dumbbell detection. | - ESP32 + IMU: motion, orientation and stability telemetry. | - Garmin Ve...

name: outcomes-body
text:
- Reusable signals for arm extension, range, symmetry, stability and exertion.
- Consistent frame-level data for HCI evaluation and future interactive applications.
- Middleware foundation for adaptive, assistive and rehabilitation-oriented systems.
- Validate sensor fusion before the final game layer.

alt: FitQuest live sensor monitor showing pose tracking, dumbbell detection and sensor telemetry

text: Hi, I am Milton Torres, a MITACS Globalink Research Intern from Mexico, supervised by Dr. Rita Orji, Dr. Fidelia Orji and Dr. Grace Ataguba. FitQuest addresses the challenge of representing physical movement reliably across heterogeneous sensors. The methodology combines camera-based YOLO pose and dumbbell detection, ESP32 IMU telemetry, and Garmin heart-rate context. The expected outcome is a reusable set of normalized movement signals for HCI evaluation and future adaptive, assistive and rehabilitation-oriented applications.
